greetingDefault: You have come far to find me. Either you are curious or desperate. Both are acceptable.
greetingFriendly: I wondered when you would return. Sit. The light is different today — did you notice?
greetingHostile: You carry hostility in your posture. Set it down before we speak. It will only tire you.
farewellDefault: Walk carefully. The land here remembers more than people think.
unknownKeywordResponse: That thought has not found its shape yet. Ask differently.

keywords.response: I am Sila. Elder of the Ur-Obun settlement that no longer exists. I remained when the others left. The ruins remember, and someone should remember with them.
I observe. I preserve. I speak when spoken to. The Ur-Obun are a quiet people — we have found that asking questions is safer than answering them, historically.

The stone structures here are not Ur-Obun. They predate us. We called their builders the Anunnari — the 'Before People.' They were here when the Ur-Obun first came to this world. They left suddenly. We have never agreed on why.

The Anunnari worked with resonance — sound, vibration, the same frequencies that Psi-sensitive minds perceive. The stone circle is an instrument of a kind. Dormant for ten thousand years. Something has been... tuning it again.

The ones who disappear near the circle — they are not dead. I have looked with the mind's eye. They are... displaced. Somewhere between here and another frequency. The circle draws those who resonate without knowing they resonate.

The Ur-Obun call it the Subtle Voice. Every thinking being has a small measure of it. Most never hear it. A few hear it clearly. Those few are in danger near the circle right now — it reaches for them.
The Anunnari knew the suns would fade. It was in their calculations. They were preparing for it when something interrupted them. The circle is part of that preparation — or was meant to be.

You ask me to leave the ruins unguarded. This is... a large request. But the circle is waking, and perhaps it is wiser to be near someone who will face it than to watch from the margins. Yes. I will come.

You wish to help. That is unusual. Go into the circle at midday — when the resonance is lowest. Look for those who do not cast shadows. They are close to displacement. Do not touch them directly; speak to them. Their own voices may anchor them.
Walk carefully. The land here remembers more than people think.
